title: dalenia glandulosa
intro: Dalenia glandulosa, also known as 'Tree Violet', is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Dalbergia genus, native to tropical regions in Africa and Asia. The medicinal properties of Dalenia glandulosa lie in its roots, which contain alkaloids that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its use as an antidote for snake bites and insect stings. Dalenia glandulosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties and alleviate various health issues.
